label: Hall corrections at finite d_i/R regularize the H3 singularity over a layer of thickness d_i
description: The H3 quartic minimum of F(r) is a singular feature of ideal MHD. At finite ion skin depth d_i, Hall corrections regularize this singularity over a layer of thickness ~d_i (Shiraishi 2005). For compact pinches with d_i/R~0.1, this is a regime change rather than a perturbation. Recent work (Mahajan-Lingam 2024, Sainterme-Ebrahimi 2026) characterizes the Hall-MHD wave spectrum as fundamentally distinct from MHD at d_i scales, with global whistler instabilities growing faster than ideal-MHD modes. Atlas's framework provides the unperturbed reference against which Hall corrections are to be computed in companion work.

scope.regime: finite d_i/R, Hall-MHD regime around H3 layer
scope.framework: Hall MHD, symmetrizable but non-Hermitian operator

contributing_claims.label: Hall effect regularizes the Alfvén singularity over a layer of thickness ~d_i
Hall MHD waves are fundamentally distinct from MHD waves at d_i scales
In Hall regime, global whistler instabilities grow significantly faster than ideal-MHD modes
Below SARI bound M_A<1, ideal-MHD m=1 growth rate is exactly zero in asymptotic d_i/R→0 regime
H3 quartic Alfvén minimum is the structural ingredient absent from shear-flow-only treatments

contributing_evidence.label: Hall-MHD solutions converge to ideal MHD outside d_i-scale neighborhood of singularity
Global whistler instabilities grow significantly faster than ideal-MHD modes at d_i/L ~ few%

open_questions.question: Does the n^-4 accumulation survive at finite d_i, or is it replaced by a Hall-modified spectrum on scale d_i?
open_questions.feeds_into: Atlas's own caveat; this question is essentially the bridge between the asymptotic result and any operational pinch

suggested_next_steps.description: Run Legolas with Hall current extension (De Jonghe et al. 2022) on RIGID-BELTRAMI-A across d_i/R∈[0, 0.1]; track spectral accumulation exponent
Derive matched-asymptotic Hall-MHD correction to the H3 indicial equation
Sainterme-Ebrahimi 2026 vs Atlas: do whistler modes appear in the Atlas-stable parameter region or only outside it?

open_questions.question: At what d_i/R does the global whistler instability cross the Atlas-stable region?

suggested_next_steps.description: Parametric Hall-MHD eigenvalue scan vs d_i/R, M_A, β

transitions_out.relation: boundary case — Atlas's d_i/R→0 is the unperturbed reference
competing instability — both are non-ideal mechanisms that can defeat Atlas-stable regime
